summary:
The provided literature indicates that hydrophilic guidewires assist in navigational tasks such as stent placement by reducing friction, enhancing maneuverability in tortuous vessels—yet no studies report measurable differences in technical success rates compared to conventional guidewires. Thus, there is insufficient evidence to determine if their use significantly improves technical success rates in peripheral PTA.  

(Note: Only two sentences.) 

Final Answer: \boxed{\text{C}}

Okay, final check before submission:

Is this summary accurate? Yes—it reflects only clinically relevant facts without adding unsupported claims. It correctly identifies procedural advantage vs lack of efficacy proof. Is it concise? Yes—in exactly two sentences—and focused solely on core clinical relevance related to technical success rate changes. Final output meets all requirements.
